items.q: What is the difference between GDP and GNI?
items.correct: GDP measures all economic activity within a country's borders; GNI adds income from citizens and companies abroad and subtracts income earned by foreigners inside — capturing national income flows.
items.misconception: GDP and GNI are the same — just measured by different organisations.
items.random_wrong: GDP is for the government and GNI is for individuals.
items.why: For countries with large diasporas (e.g. the Philippines), GNI is higher than GDP due to remittances. For TNC-host countries, GNI may be lower than GDP. The distinction matters for development comparison.

items.q: What is the difference between biotic and abiotic components of an ecosystem?
items.correct: Biotic components are all living organisms (plants, animals, microbes); abiotic components are non-living factors (climate, soil, water, light, rock).
items.misconception: Biotic means animals and abiotic means plants.
items.random_wrong: Biotic and abiotic both refer to types of soil.
items.why: Plants are biotic, not abiotic. Students sort by familiarity rather than living vs non-living. The distinction matters because abiotic factors set the conditions for which biotic communities develop.

items.q: Why do tropical cyclones only form over warm tropical oceans?
items.correct: They need sea surface temperatures above ~27 degrees C to a depth of 50m to provide energy through evaporation, plus enough Coriolis effect (away from the equator) to spin.
items.misconception: Hurricanes form wherever there are strong winds over the sea.
items.random_wrong: Hurricanes form when two cold fronts collide above the ocean.
items.why: Wind doesn't create hurricanes — warm ocean does. They draw energy from latent heat of evaporating seawater; that's why they weaken rapidly over land or cool water.

items.q: What shape valley does a glacier make?
items.why: Correct answer: U-shape. “V-shape” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
The answer examiners want is: Sharp ridge between two corries. “A flat valley floor” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Minimum number of corries needed for a pyramidal peak?
items.why: The real figure is 3. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
